name: Chocolate Mint Melt-Aways
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by: Bon Appetit, Dec. 1990
Cookies:
Preheat oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.
Butter 2 large heavy baking sheets.
Using a mixer, cream butter, powdered sugar, and extracts until light.
Beat in half of the flour.
Stir in remaining flour.
Spoon half of dough into a pastry bag fitted with #4 star tip or cookie press. Either pipe 2 1/2 inch ovals with solid centers or press into desired shape, spacing cookies 1/2 inch apart.
Repeat with remaining dough.
Bake until golden brown on edges, about 12 minutes.
Transfer to paper towels and cool.
Ganache:
Bring cream and butter to a simmer in a heavy saucepan over medium heat. Add chocolate and stir until smooth. Mix in extract. Refrigerate just until firm enough to spread, stirring occasionally, about 30 minutes.
Line 2 cookie sheets with foil or parchment paper.
Using a small spatula, spread 1 tablespoon of ganache over the flat side of each cookie. Arrange ganache side up on cookie sheets. Refrigerate until firm.
Coating:
Melt chocolate and shortening together over double boiler until smooth. Remove from water.
Grasping cookie by the edges, dip bottom of cookie into chocolate, place on a cookie sheet with the chocolate side down.
Repeat with remaining cookies.
Chill until chocolate sets.
